mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate declines by more than 25 basis points over a 3-month window, the reduction in short-term borrowing costs triggers a compression of equity risk premiums and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% as of Sept 2015 indicates near-zero rates
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle or post-crisis accommodation
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) of at least 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market momentum and consumer income growth, which translates into increased aggregate demand and positive equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142,371 thousand as of Sept 2015
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with full employment approaching
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth months produce positive 60-day returns in fewer than 58% of instances, or correlation between PAYEMS growth rate and forward 60-day returns falls below 0.10 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in real retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 2% month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate profit expectations, which depresses S&P 500 valuations and produces negative forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices earnings forecasts downward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 447,686 million dollars as of Sept 2015
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 5-day returns in fewer than 52% of cases, or the mechanism fails to predict direction correctly in more than 48% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) contracts month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) simultaneously rises by more than 0.2 percentage points, this joint signal indicates supply-side weakness coupled with labor market deterioration, triggering a negative equity market repricing over 20 trading days as recession risk increases.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.08 and UNRATE at 5.1% as of Sept 2015
failure_condition: Joint trigger instances produce negative 20-day returns in fewer than 56% of cases, or the interaction effect (measured as the coefficient on INDPRO×UNRATE interaction term) is not statistically significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1,100 thousand units annualized, combined with positive month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS), signals robust housing demand and consumer spending momentum that supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations over a 60-day forward horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1100 thousand units AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,126 thousand units as of Aug 2015
regime_hint: post-crisis housing recovery and demand-led expansion
failure_condition: Joint trigger instances produce positive 60-day returns in fewer than 60% of cases, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.10 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2% year-over-year while the federal funds rate stays near zero, the central bank maintains accommodative policy that sustains low equity discount rates and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 237.566 and FEDFUNDS at 0.14% as of Sept 2015
failure_condition: Joint trigger instances produce positive 20-day returns in fewer than 57% of cases, or the mechanism fails when inflation expectations rise above 2.5% despite low policy rates.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.2% while nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) decelerates to below 100,000 jobs per month, this dual signal indicates labor market softening that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 5.2% AND month-over-month PAYEMS growth < 100,000 jobs
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.1% and PAYEMS at 142,371 thousand as of Sept 2015
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or demand-side weakness
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 5-day returns in fewer than 54% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over two consecutive months signals weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side contraction, which depresses corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day forward horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines in both current month and prior month
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of cases, or the mechanism fails to predict direction correctly in more than 48% of triggered windows over any 2-year rolling period.
